en.extract:
Ussher's flycatcher (Artomyias ussheri) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.  It is found in Ivory Coast, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Nigeria, and Sierra Leone.  Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. 


Taxonomy
Ussher's flycatcher was formally described in 1871 by the English ornithologist Richard Bowdler Sharpe based on a specimen collected at Abrobonko in present-day Ghana, by the British colonial administrator Herbert Taylor Ussher, who became Governor of the Gold Coast. Sharpe coined the current binomial name Artomyias ussheri where the specific epithet was named after the collector. The type locality, Abrobonko, is between Cape Coast and Elmina on the lower Sweet (Kakum) river. The species is monotypic: no subspecies are recognised. It was formerly placed in the genus Bradornis.

'''Ussher's flycatcher''' ('''''Artomyias ussheri''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]].  It is found in [[Ivory Coast]], [[Ghana]], [[Guinea]], [[Guinea-Bissau]], [[Liberia]], [[Nigeria]], and [[Sierra Leone]].  Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. 

==Taxonomy==
Ussher's flycatcher was [[species description|formally described]] in 1871 by the English ornithologist [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe]] based on a specimen collected at Abrobonko in present-day Ghana, by the British colonial administrator [[Herbert Taylor Ussher]], who became [[List of governors of the Gold Coast|Governor of the Gold Coast]]. Sharpe coined the current [[binomial name]] ''Artomyias ussheri'' where the specific epithet was named after the collector.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Sharpe | first1=R. Bowdler | author1-link=Richard Bowdler Sharpe | date=1871 | title=On seven new or lately described species of African birds | journal=Ibis | series=3rd series |volume=1 | pages=414–417 [416–417] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/8587928 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | pages=324–325 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14484025 }}</ref> The [[type locality (biology)|type locality]], Abrobonko, is between [[Cape Coast]] and [[Elmina]] on the lower Sweet (Kakum) river.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Dowsett | first=R.J. | date=2005 | title=A supplementary gazetteer for the birds of Ghana | journal=Malimbus | volume=27 | pages=116–119 | url=https://www.malimbus.org/en/contents/articles/V27/27116119.pdf }}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]]: no [[subspecies]] are recognised. It was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Bradornis]]''.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/chats/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=28 March 2025}}</ref>
